license_notes: synthetic derivative of alice-two-story-house via palette swap
title: Alice two-story house (synthetic industrial)
description:
This two-story industrial-style house presents a rectangular block silhouette, measuring 13 by 15 units at its base and rising 21 units high. The exterior walls are primarily constructed from spruce planks, giving them a warm brown hue, complemented by cobblestone accents and a strip of dirt and grass blocks at the base, creating a subtle plinth effect. The roof is a simple gable design, formed from a combination of cobblestone and spruce planks, with exposed stripped spruce logs forming structural elements. Small glass pane windows are visible on the lower level, providing glimpses into the interior. The overall aesthetic is rustic and functional, characteristic of industrial design.

The interior layout suggests a functional division between the two stories, with the ground floor likely serving as common areas and the upper floor for more private spaces, typical of residential structures. The house features multiple rooms across its two levels. Circulation within the house is facilitated by internal stairs, visible in the partially open structure. Lighting is provided by small glass pane windows and lanterns, suggesting a moderately lit interior, balancing natural light with artificial illumination. The use of stripped spruce logs and cobblestone throughout the structure reinforces the industrial, almost utilitarian, character of the building, evoking a sense of sturdy, unpretentious living.
